pages.notes: Page is blank. Partial gutter text visible at bottom-right edge is a continuation of the right page's handwritten note and is transcribed there.

pages.full_text:
Saint Anne Room, Beaune, former linen room, P 20,

Tapastry, miracle of Saint Eloi. Beaune.

Youth hostel & camping, Lorde valley.
04.71.23.56.
10/06.07.88.
19.04.
15110 [?Fridefont]
Auvergne
Ferme Auberge.
photo_captions: Saint Anne Room, Beaune, former linen room, P 20,
Tapastry, miracle of Saint Eloi. Beaune.
pages.notes: Page contains two glued-in photographs and one postcard/illustration. Top photo shows a room with mannequins in period dress; caption reads 'Saint Anne Room, Beaune, former linen room, P 20,'. Middle photo shows a tapestry with a rabbit; caption reads 'Tapastry, miracle of Saint Eloi. Beaune.' Bottom item is a printed postcard/illustration of 'Ferme Auberge des deux vallées' (text printed on the card, not handwritten). Bottom-left handwritten note gives contact/address details: youth hostel and campsite in the Lorde valley, phone number 04.71.23.56., a number sequence '10/06.07.88.' (could be phone number or date reference — unclear), postcode 15110, place name read as '[?Fridefont]' (possibly Fridefont, Cantal; could also read 'FrideSont'), Auvergne, Ferme Auberge. The '10/06.07.88.' string is ambiguous — could be a partial phone number or a date; year 88 would pre-date this volume (2003–2019) so more likely a phone number fragment.
